We stayed at Hotel Sarang Palace for 3 nights in the last week of November, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. The room was clean, well-maintained, and equipped with all the basic amenities we needed for a comfortable stay. Cleanliness throughout the property was impressive, and the staff were courteous, helpful, and always willing to assist.

The breakfast spread was good with a decent variety to choose from. However, the dinner food could use some improvement in terms of taste and overall quality.

Despite that, we had a comfortable stay and feel the hotel offers good value for money. Overall, I would rate it 4 out of 5 and would recommend it to others.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Namasvi Salot","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIyBMUfHFoAs29_v_DfLWLv1tzVwT3vW-V9RRXL7AzNFlpJcw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The rooms had a decent ambience, but the overall experience was extremely disappointing due to poor service and unreasonable policies. The room itself had several issues. The window did not close properly, allowing cold air to constantly enter. The rooms are extremely close to each other, and noise travels very easily. Guests were loud late at night, making it difficult to rest.

Adding to this, the hotel advertises a beautiful terrace in its photos, but when we tried to access it, we were told it was private and were asked to leave. This feels misleading.

We stayed for 3 night at the hotel, location of Hotel is very convenient and facilities including Swiming pool, Game Zone, Gym, Rooftop seating area and Complimentary Massage chair provided at budgeted rate make our stay experience very good. We stayed in Standard room which was also enough sufficient for family of 3.
Three Issue that I feel needs to be improved are:-
1) Behaviour of Staff - All of them at the reception were rude and unrespectful, specially the lady as if they are favouring letting us stay in the hotel. Being in hospitality industry, they should behave with customer politely and in respectful manner.
2) Bath kit in the room compromises only of 2 towels, 1 small medimix and 1 rs Shampoo sachet. Basic things like toothpaste, Bodywash, Napkins, Comb, Hair Oil are missing which make our stay a bit uncomfortable. They didn't even provide complimentary Water bottle. A proper kit should be provided to Customers.
3) Room service - I was not very satisfied with Room service. They said that instead of Complimentary water bottle, they provide RO water jar. I had ask them to send it as I had no water with me, delivering for which they taken almost 30 minutes with almost 3-4 reminders required. This happened with me twice after which I had stopped ordering anything from Room service and started bringing my own bottle purchased from outside.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vishal Arya","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX70qSLyLs7sFSevTsH1NpjzUaTwN8GDe2cgXAvPX50ikaN2WH-=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"I stayed at Hotel Arco Palace, Jaipur for one day in a standard room for leisure purposes.

The check-in experience was slightly delayed due to heavy crowd at the reception. The staff mentioned that the delay was caused by a late checkout by previous guests. Sarita at the reception was polite and cooperative, but the overall process felt a bit tedious and could have been handled more efficiently. Also, there was no staff available to assist with luggage, which was inconvenient after a long journey.

The standard room was average—neither very good nor very bad. There were visible moisture patches on multiple walls, which affected the overall feel of the room. The bathroom was acceptable but not as clean as expected. One thing that stood out was the absence of complimentary packaged water bottles. On asking room service, I was informed that RO water is provided only on request, which was a little unexpected.

Staff behavior was generally good, especially at the reception. Apart from Sarita, my interaction with other staff members was minimal.

The food experience deserves special mention. We had lunch at the restaurant, and the food was excellent—fresh, tasty, and well-prepared. Room service food was also good. However, the morning breakfast buffet was average and did not match the quality of the food served during the day, which was slightly disappointing.

Parking was full but seemed spacious enough. I did not use other facilities like the pool, spa, or Wi-Fi.

The location is a major advantage. The hotel is around 1 km from Jaipur Railway Station, and most major tourist attractions are within a 5–10 km radius, making it quite convenient for travelers.

Overall Verdict

Hotel Arco Palace offers good value for money, especially for standard room bookings considering the location and inclusion of breakfast. With faster check-in, better room maintenance, and improved breakfast quality, the experience could be significantly better.

Recommended for budget travelers looking for a decent stay near the railway station.
Local IDs are not allowed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCHH50bhNT8hzLMVhhJEEhg&fid=0x0:0x8d5ee34e1aebac5a"],"When":"2025-12-16"},{"Name":"charanpreet singh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjULq_HcErfIDDGu3z_0SNonEpbszvfZYjPn_yKsJFy_YtyqVkbc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I am really not satisfied with the hotel arco palace  overall. They charged me a good amount during the season but the expectations did not meet as how it was shown.
I had to get my room change as the first room was not that good which they had given me.
The service is quite slow. No water bottles provided. The waiter came with a water jug asked me to pour it into the kettle provided in the room. I don’t believe this practice is followed in such kind of hotels.
Location of hotel is in busy traffic area , quite hard to find.
Bathroom showers were not working efficiently , water was not draining out properly. When this issue was addressed to them, the person at the reception came up with an excuse that the maintenance work is going on which is obviously not a good reason to tell a customer.
If the customer is reaching at your hotel during season break, then I hope that everything is up to the mark.

Few things which I liked here -

Breakfast was good.
If you are coming by car they have a good parking space.
Market is nearby the hotel.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vishal Bhatnagar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV0YZ1npvvEsaTNiEYCbue9H-GnH_dO02R8XchAOLLtEf4Rvw1i=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Over all values for money accommodation....
Coperative staff as we require one additional room on request they provide us with no additional cost..
For that thanks ...
My only concern is that you apply so many rues but in hotel industry it's not so mandatory...
Alwys try to be flexible on rules as quest would come again ....","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"nusrat gori","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPqHBXju9UMpudQehpkU1apTIo-fCVr8LPqFA42mueHsP52w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During my visit to Hotel Arco Palace, I initially found the check-in area quite crowded — it almost felt as if they were offering free rooms to everyone! However, once I completed the check-in process and explored the hotel, I understood why it was so busy.

The hotel offers an impressive range of amenities at a very affordable price, including a swimming pool, gym, game zone, rooftop seating area, and even massage chairs for relaxation. The overall hospitality truly feels like that of a five-star property, making it an excellent value for money.

Hotel Arco Palace provides comfort, quality, and great service — all within a budget-friendly range. Truly a wonderful experience and highly recommended for anyone visiting the area.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Madhumita Majumder","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVYcKEVhsdWXBuSra91jvSaXVwcjvcYcc9RN1sw7rU_u3txSW7m=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Just superb! user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Sukamal Chatterjee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKZaF59XYIX6o0hyVZ8cx1xUSJFo-EXIo9L6s8SHlse6yHfAn8=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I stayed in this hotel from 16-20 Dec 2025. The experience was wonderful. The room and bathroom were clean and hygienic. The room service as well as the restaurant services were extremely prompt and professional. The staff were very courteous and respectful. Every small request was responded to with care and promptness. The location of the hotel is probably the best in the city with every sightseeing spot being in a range of 20-30 min. Bapu bazar and other shopping areas were right next to the hotel. Overall a very fulfilling experience and full value for money. I would strongly recommend this hotel for anyone coming to Jaipur.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Neel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU3ZDdzuCy0VdO7MPiBgTWzvh_pZPPWV4GJ33CDeSHfkSmf_j3-=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It was very good experience. I would recommend anyone travelling to Jaipur to take a stay here. We had booked 6 rooms for 12 of us & this was the experience -

Staff - 5/5 - The staff is very polite & helpful. We had ~25 bags which they helped us carry to & fro our vehicle. Any minor inconvenience was taken care of promptly.

Location - 5/5 - The tourist places, shopping bazaars are quite accessible from this place as it's located in the midst of the city.

Cleanliness - 4/5 - Out of 6 rooms, 4 we're quite clean. 2 of the rooms we received had heavy smoking smell which did not go away despite room cleaning & room freshener multiple times. That was the only bad experience.

Experience - 5/5 - Easy check in & check out. Good Haveli types vibes. No biased experience just because many foreigners stay there. Would 100% recommend this place. My whole family loved it.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"seanmN9234SA","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeSe9ibNVY65MpgeQh70I370foM0-7tEPyVc9CIfSq5Ekli093sZJVPBoLD5d5qPK_2rw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Positive feedback would be that it felt very clean. Had a good size room with comfortable bed and powerful shower. The staff were friendly and accommodating, especially in the restaurant and the older gentlemen on the door was helpful finding cabs and autos. It had a nice restaurant. I was able to leave my big bag with them after checking out. Nice decoration in the foyer and rooms and good location. ||Not so positive feedback would be that although generally friendly and helpful, the larger gentleman behind reception told me what the bill was and then the figure it would be with his tip included. I laughed at the time not knowing what to say (I'm British, that's how we handle awkwardness 😁) but when it came to paying he gave me approx 250 - 300 rupees less in change. I said does this include tip, he said no, then I started counting it and he said yes. I accepted it as I was in a rush to get to the train station. I forgot to ask whether the tip was shared with all staff. I gave the gentlemen at the door a tip as I genuinely appreciated his help and it was my choice. It made sense to me afterwards that this is why he accepts payment at the end and the card payment doesn't work. The stay was very comfortable I would just be aware of this method of receiving tips.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjURUEyFTDXINVyrcXL-oesRNW1-BthsJgtn-KX-XBXZq-KnRSc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Though it looks like a budget hotel, the final price came closer to rates of hotels like ginger. [{"Name":"Amit Jaiswal","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXbCGHXzEetpZQz96NlY-5yM-yZNRsTbvEVQ5ojUiZPoKx-vu_L=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had an excellent experience staying here! Even though we checked in at midnight, the process was completely seamless. I had informed the hotel about a late check-in, and they went the extra mile by following up when we didn’t arrive at the communicated time — a thoughtful gesture that showed genuine care.

The rooms were clean, hygienic, and spacious. I’d highly recommend opting for the deluxe room, which comes with a balcony and made the stay even more enjoyable. The location is convenient with a few eateries nearby, and to top it off, they even gave a small gift on arrival — a very pleasant surprise.

Overall, a wonderful stay and definitely a place I’d recommend.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sriram Medepalli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJosyMzSKh0W_rgaOxVPmvgNyJlh-xLGdLXb-H9XVPVMqcv_w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Our stay was made extremely pleasant by the Stafff’s attitude, behaviour and service. The room was mentioned as 180 sq. ft., and it felt quite decent in size and comfort. Overall, the stay was pleasant — the staff was polite, cooperative, and quick to assist whenever we needed something.The hotel also provided basic amenities, including a kettle in the room (though it’s only suitable for boiling water). The location in Bani Park is convenient with shops and eateries within 100 meters. One of the best parts of our stay was discovering a small food stall behind the hotel that served delicious momos for just ₹60 — a tasty surprise!In summary, Hotel Reeve Inn is a budget-friendly place with good service, clean rooms, and a great location — for families looking for a comfortable stay in Jaipur.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nargis Liyakat Ali","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqvT3VJX0uBb57Wq74Ga2VKhaKBwh1xy0XOijzFXijcox4tw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Although I usually don’t like writing reviews, this time our trip was quite long - two weeks in total - during which we visited Delhi, Ajmer, and Jaipur. We visited this hotel on December 10 and checked out on December 17, staying for seven days. We spent six days in a family room and the last day in a deluxe room. The food was fresh and delicious, and it truly felt like home-cooked meals - special thanks to Sushanto ji and the cook for the wonderful food. Any room-related issues were resolved quickly, and the hotel managers, Sandeep and the other two managers, were very kind and welcoming.

Highly recommended for a stay in Jaipur. There is zero security, making it unsafe for anyone, especially families and couples. The staff is rude, unprofessional, and only interested in scamming guests with hidden charges. Though I am a little late because I wanted to take time to provide feedback to this (C9) OYO hotel in Jaipur.
I took a decision to stay this hotel for 5 days. But truly saying I have spent two days only. Because the service that  I have received, was very bad and low.
The first day room, I got that was very smelly and in the washroom, I was not able to enter. I complaint them about the room. They changed my room where I have observed, there was no door lock and answer I received from management: Sir, nobody will enter into your room. We will change the room by tomorrow morning. I said what, how can I believe in you that nobody will come into my room and what if I would lose my things. The Guy was addressing me was not even wearing sleeper. Because if they had no any other option and it was too late, so I have decided to stay at the same smelly room. And now in the mid of the night light went off and nobody bothered to come to me. I went to the reception and everybody was snoring, I wake them up they heard my complaint and slept again and again nobody cared. Again, I went to them, they changed my room to deluxe room that made me happy Atelast they felt something that I am the customer and a human as well. Well, not for so long they did not want me to stay happily there. So, they again changed my room. I did complain to the owner. Even he did not care. So then I realized if the owner does not care why his staff will bother. The owner gave me an excuse, though there was an issue with the electricity, but We cannot give you any compensation. You cannot stay happily in this room (sarcastic) we want to ruin your trip so please change the room to early one. You have to change it now. In two days I have changed three rooms.

Talk about the free breakfast which I had purchased.

First day - Poha with full of spices. Wow. I started my day with burning tongue.
Second day- Fully black roasted dry Aloo Prantha with double thick thrust and full of spicy and I asked for curd and that was chargeable. What a money making plan.

2 days three rooms. I missed my some stuff which I never got back.

Third Day I have changed my hotel to another one.

OYO helpline - I called them first day when the light went off. Agent placed my call on hold and disconnect. Second time I have called. I received answer we will talk to the owner. Nobody has bothered to take the action at the same time.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Looking forward to staying here again!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Manan Panwar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXJFJEcfHaKJwDt136G-cyzlDe2dDmLQatd7uthLkeFE4iYfQfgmQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Title: Worst Experience Ever – Hotel Akash Inn is a Fraud & Cheating Hotel

I booked a room at Hotel Akash Inn (direct booking via phone) on 2nd August 2025 for 3 days – 2 days triple occupancy and 1 day double occupancy. I paid ₹5,220/- in advance for triple occupancy. On 3rd August, one of my friends had to urgently leave, so I immediately informed the hotel to shift us to double occupancy. The hotel agreed and clearly told us that the difference amount will be refunded at checkout.

At the time of checkout, since we were in a hurry, we requested the hotel to transfer the difference online. They agreed, but till today (29th September 2025) there has been no refund, no bill, no response to calls or WhatsApp messages. The staff is deliberately ignoring and cheating guests.

At first, I thought the small refund amount would come within 7 working days as per hotel standards, but it has now been almost two months. This is not about the amount anymore—it’s about cheating customers and unethical practices.

👉 This hotel survives mostly on event companies who push bookings here because they get cheap deals, while the guests are fooled with fake good ratings. In reality, the service is pathetic, dishonest, and shameful. With so many reputed hotels nearby, I strongly advise foreign travelers and Indian guests to avoid this hotel at all costs.

I am sharing screenshots of my payment, WhatsApp chats, and proof to show how they shamelessly duped us.

⚠️ DO NOT BOOK HOTEL AKASH INN if you don’t want to face the same harassment and fraud.

Outstanding Stay at The Pink Palace, Jaipur

Our stay at The Pink Palace, Jaipur was absolutely wonderful and truly memorable. The service provided by the hotel was excellent from start to finish, and every staff member made us feel welcomed and cared for.

A special mention to Mr. Sandeep Chaudhary ji at the reception, who is extremely friendly, polite, and always ready to help with a warm smile. His hospitality made our experience even more pleasant. Also, Mr. Gautam ji from Room Service deserves appreciation for his well-behaved nature, prompt service, and courteous attitude.

The food was delicious, hygienic, and well-prepared, adding great value to our stay. Every meal felt fresh and comforting, just like home-cooked food.

What truly sets this hotel apart is the homely atmosphere—it honestly feels like staying with family rather than at a hotel. Comfortable rooms, great food, excellent service, and kind people make The Pink Palace a perfect place to stay in Jaipur.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for comfort, warmth, and genuine hospitality in the Pink City. 💖🏨✨

In all this the time was 1 am in night and we was so exhausted, finally want to take bath and sleep after long journey , and then we find that there was no hot water as there was no personal geyser , so we sleep as it is . In morning we think that hot water will come but no there water geyser was not working and we took bath from cold water in month of  November and leave that bullshit , i would rate it in minus if it was possible, worst experience , they should shut there hotel down as there are no basic facilities and they charge so high for this , please avoid if you don’t want to spoil your trip","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gunjan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUS2Pbrd22fH5Qhh4DHyubOgrilysXL-4yKcXsIyi8uWLrdCcm5WQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"To all the people reading this, my recommendation to you would be to skip this hotel completely. It’s not worth of your time, energy and money.
They seriously don’t understand the meaning of service and hospitality, I had a horrible experience with my family.
Please read about my experience below:
I reached the hotel around 8 pm at night and on entering my room I see cockroaches in my room and it was disgusting. When we told this to the hotel staff, they were very casual about it said hit spray kar dete hain but wasn’t all this supposed to be done before the arrival of the guest. If they were to do the pest control that same time, where were we supposed to go at night and when we questioned them they said ‘kya karein sir aap btao’. No seriousness, no apology, nothing. We did the basic cleaning ourselves and sat down and asked for a bottle of water around 8:30 and waited for 1 hour but no one came. We left out  for dinner and came back and were then given the bottle around 11pm that too after reminding them. We asked for the menu for breakfast as it was not in the room to which they said their printer was not working. I mean wasn’t it supposed to be in the room already. Why do these ppl had to wait for someone to ask for it and then realise their printer not working. The condition of the washroom was not very clean and it had leakage and they did not even had a plumber to repair it. We had our room booked with breakfast where they served parantha, poha and bread-butter and in that too they charged extra for the curd they served with the parantha. I mean how are we supposed to eat a parantha then without the curd. If you serving an item atleast serve it properly. I don’t mind paying for my food according to my choice but if my breakfast was included in my room booking then it is supposed to be there properly.
One day we were tired and didn’t wanted to step out for dinner so thought of ordering it in house but we didn’t had the menu. The landline of the room wasn’t working. We went down to again ask for the menu to which they said today they won’t be serving dinner. Later the other one said it’ll be late, both having different things to say. When we called the manager to inform for the denial of the services and the staff behaviour, the manager was very rude and said ‘maine toh aapka room book nhi kiya, jisne kiya hai aap usse baat karo’. I mean what the hell! Who the hell made you the manager and what अरे you supposed to do if not helping your guests!
We had called the owner while our booking and hence reached out to him at last. The owner at-least acknowledged our concerns, unlike the other staff, and apologised. He scolded his staff over phone, after which the behaviour and attitude of the staff changed. But even that wasn’t very long lived.
Overall delayed service, ignorant behaviour of the staff and always making excuses.
When we are out with family or friends in a new place, we rely on a good stay for services, comfort and guidance.
I did not had an option to give star rating in zero or negative but this is not worth the amount we are paying.
I would never recommend this place to anyone so please skip this place for your stay in Jaipur. And if the owner is reading this, thank you for acknowledging our concerns and request you to please do something for your other guests. Anyone who’s coming there they deserve a good behaviour and service.
Thanks!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bharat Gome","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIfrBvE3gqX4hCOxWEluUHRxkgdE1bf7AESjBcuaJurALr_ZA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nice location with nice staff behaviour also nice service strongly recommend to all pls visit this place it's near jaipur junction railway station and Sindhi camp bus stand","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"kartik singhal","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL5XhMnOfKFo5U1rWS_vQ7tahk5U6UDNccH-8CEd7TJC44vDA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"On a trip of 3 days to jaipur the best experience was by the hotel and staff. The elevator also gets stuck sometimes. So, it's just an average place to stay, but it's expensive because of the location. Oh, and there's no parking, so you have to park on the street. I appreciate your response. However, I will not be removing my review as I have proof of paying an electricity bill that exceeded ₹5,000 for a month. Additionally, I was charged ₹15 per unit, which should not have been more than ₹7.65 per unit.

I had one of the worst hotel experiences at SPOT ON Angle Paradise. I had booked through Booking.com and had a confirmed price, but when I arrived, the person at reception — Sahadev — refused to honour the booking price and forcefully made me pay an extra ₹1500.

The rooms were not clean, the water supply was poor, and the overall maintenance was below basic standards. What should have been a simple family stay became a hassle from start to finish.

The staff were polite and helpful. Overall had a good experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Navneet kumar rai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxYOffPfk_DKc-X1uCWLUU6qBQj1RnkqujatgC3kmKonUWt0DE3w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The air conditioning was a major issue during my stay. It only had an on/off function, with no option to adjust the temperature. Despite repeated requests to have it fixed, nothing was done. The staff were unhelpful and unresponsive.

Access to drinking water was another big problem. Even though it was included in the cost, I had to ask multiple times just to get basic drinking water.

The overall environment felt neglected. The locality appeared run-down, and the surroundings didn’t feel clean or comfortable.

The check-in and check-out timings (1:00 p.m. and 11:00 a.m.) seemed designed to encourage extra charges for early check-ins or slightly late departures, which felt unfair given the overall quality.

They also charged extra for a “garden view” room, but the view wasn’t worth the additional cost at all.

Food options were extremely limited—basically just Maggi and tea. That’s unacceptable for a hotel charging premium rates.

The location was a huge disappointment. It was far more remote than advertised. I had chosen the property thinking it would be convenient for sightseeing, but in reality, it was hard to get cabs and travel anywhere.

The only slightly positive aspect was the interior cleanliness, which was okay—not great, but not terrible.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBDp61jAcEGJyAmJlapCyh7&fid=0x0:0x778dfa014034f83b"],"When":"2025-7-30"},{"Name":"Neha Maharana","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL4zi0xkKfC5D6OQwLaHfBHSrM7SjM5swjxSTh2zBfunsG8qg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Poor Cooperation: The owner and staff were not cooperating.
